fields_json: {"name":"Jason Murray","bio":"Backend engineer in Seattle building Ridgeline, a self-hosted read-it-later tool for developers. Writes about Go, PostgreSQL, and running things on a homelab Kubernetes cluster.","twitter":"@jmurray_dev"}

{"title":"Introducing Ridgeline: A Self-Hosted Read-It-Later for Developers","slug":"introducing-ridgeline","body":"I kept losing good engineering articles in browser tabs and bookmark folders I never reopened. Ridgeline is my answer: a self-hosted bookmarking and read-it-later tool built in Go and PostgreSQL, with a React frontend. This first post covers why I built it and what the early roadmap looks like.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-go-backend"}},"published":true}

{"title":"A Tour of the Ridgeline Go Backend","slug":"go-backend-tour","body":"Ridgeline's backend is a single Go binary that serves the JSON API, handles background fetching, and talks to PostgreSQL. In this post I walk through the package layout, how I structure handlers and services, and why I avoided a heavyweight framework in favor of net/http and chi.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-go-backend"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Self-Hosting Ridgeline on a Homelab Kubernetes Cluster","slug":"selfhost-kubernetes","body":"My homelab runs a small k3s cluster, and that's where Ridgeline lives. This guide covers the manifests, the ingress setup behind Cloudflare, persistent volumes for Postgres, and how I roll out new versions without downtime.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-selfhost"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Indexing PostgreSQL for Fast Bookmark Queries","slug":"postgres-indexing","body":"As my own Ridgeline library grew, listing bookmarks by tag and date started to slow down. This post walks through reading EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, choosing the right composite and partial indexes, and the measurable wins I got from each change.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-postgres"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Running Ridgeline with Docker Compose","slug":"docker-compose-guide","body":"Not everyone wants Kubernetes. The simplest way to self-host Ridgeline is a single docker-compose.yml with the app, Postgres, and a reverse proxy. This guide gives you a copy-paste setup, environment variables, and backup tips for a single-node deployment.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-docker"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Adding Full-Text Search to Ridgeline","slug":"fulltext-search","body":"Searching across saved article content is the feature people ask for most. I started with Postgres tsvector and later layered Algolia on top for instant search. This post compares both approaches and explains the indexing pipeline that keeps them in sync.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-fulltext"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Wiring Up Stripe Billing in a Go Service","slug":"stripe-billing","body":"Ridgeline offers an optional hosted tier, so I needed billing. This write-up covers the Stripe Go SDK, handling webhooks idempotently, reconciling subscription state with my own database, and the edge cases that bit me along the way.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-stripe"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Splitting Out an Internal gRPC Service in Go","slug":"grpc-internal-services","body":"The article-fetching worker got heavy enough that I pulled it into its own service and connected it over gRPC. This post covers defining the proto, generating Go code, streaming results back, and keeping the deployment simple on my homelab.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-grpc"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Provisioning My Homelab with Terraform","slug":"terraform-homelab","body":"Clicking around to set up DNS, Cloudflare rules, and VM resources got old fast. I moved as much of my homelab as I could into Terraform. This guide shows the module structure, managing state safely on a single machine, and what's worth automating versus not.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-terraform"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Adding Observability to Ridgeline with Datadog","slug":"observability-datadog","body":"Once Ridgeline had real users I needed to know when things broke before they emailed me. This post covers structured logging in Go, exporting metrics, tracing slow requests, and building a handful of Datadog dashboards and alerts that actually matter.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-observability"}},"published":true}

{"title":"A Boring, Reliable Postgres Backup Strategy","slug":"postgres-backups","body":"Backups are only useful if restores work. For Ridgeline I run nightly pg_dump to object storage with a weekly base backup and WAL archiving for point-in-time recovery. This post shares the scripts, retention policy, and how I test restores on a schedule.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-backups"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Token-Bucket Rate Limiting in Go","slug":"rate-limiting-go","body":"The public Ridgeline API needed protection from runaway scripts. I implemented a token-bucket limiter backed by Redis so it works across multiple instances. This post walks through the algorithm, the Go middleware, and how I expose limit headers to clients.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-rate-limit"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Graceful Shutdown for Go HTTP Servers","slug":"graceful-shutdown","body":"A rolling deploy that drops in-flight requests is a bad experience. This post shows how I handle SIGTERM in Ridgeline, drain the http.Server with a context deadline, finish background jobs, and close the database pool cleanly so Kubernetes rollouts are seamless.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-graceful"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Designing Multi-User Workspaces in Ridgeline","slug":"multiuser-workspaces","body":"Draft: thinking through how shared collections and per-user permissions should work without overcomplicating the data model.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-jason"}},"published":false}
{"version":"v0.1","title":"First public release","summary":"Save bookmarks via the web UI, store them in PostgreSQL, and view a simple list. Single-user only.","releaseDate":"2025-08-26"}
{"version":"v0.2","title":"Tags and folders","summary":"Added tagging, basic folders, and the ability to mark bookmarks as read or archived.","releaseDate":"2025-09-23"}
{"version":"v0.3","title":"Reader view","summary":"Introduced a distraction-free reader view that extracts article content for clean reading.","releaseDate":"2025-10-21"}
{"version":"v0.4","title":"Docker Compose deployment","summary":"Shipped an official docker-compose setup and environment-based configuration for easy self-hosting.","releaseDate":"2025-11-18"}
{"version":"v0.5","title":"Full-text search","summary":"Added full-text search over saved article content using Postgres tsvector, with an optional Algolia backend.","releaseDate":"2025-12-16"}
{"version":"v0.6","title":"Bookmark import","summary":"Import bookmarks from browser HTML exports and from Pocket and Instapaper backups.","releaseDate":"2026-01-27"}
{"version":"v0.7","title":"Optional hosted tier and billing","summary":"Launched an optional hosted plan with Stripe billing for people who don't want to self-host.","releaseDate":"2026-01-07"}
{"version":"v0.8","title":"Public API and rate limiting","summary":"Released a documented public REST API with token-bucket rate limiting and per-key quotas.","releaseDate":"2026-02-25"}

{"version":"v0.9","title":"Observability and reliability","summary":"Added structured logging, metrics, tracing, automated backups, and graceful shutdown for zero-downtime deploys.","releaseDate":"2026-10-20"}

{"version":"v1.0","title":"Ridgeline 1.0","summary":"First stable release: hardened API, browser extension, mobile-friendly reader, and a documented upgrade path. Multi-user workspaces are on the roadmap.","releaseDate":"2026-12-29"}

{"title":"Save anything to read later","description":"Capture articles, docs, and links from your browser or the API and keep them in one searchable place.","icon":"bookmark","image":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-feature-readlater"}}}

{"title":"Full-text search","description":"Find any saved article instantly, searching across titles, tags, and the full extracted content.","icon":"search","image":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-feature-search"}}}

{"title":"Self-host with full control","description":"Run Ridgeline on your own server with Docker Compose or Kubernetes. Your data stays yours.","icon":"server","image":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-feature-selfhost"}}}

{"title":"Distraction-free reader","description":"A clean reader view strips ads and clutter so you can focus on the content that matters.","icon":"book-open","image":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-screenshot-reader"}}}

{"title":"Developer-friendly API","description":"A documented REST API with rate limiting lets you build your own workflows on top of Ridgeline.","icon":"code","image":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-go-backend"}}}

{"question":"What is Ridgeline?","answer":"Ridgeline is a self-hosted read-it-later and bookmarking tool built for developers. You save links, read them in a clean view, and search across everything you've saved.","category":"General"}
{"question":"Is Ridgeline free?","answer":"Self-hosting Ridgeline is free and open source. There's an optional paid hosted plan if you'd rather not run it yourself.","category":"Pricing"}

{"question":"How do I self-host Ridgeline?","answer":"The fastest path is Docker Compose: copy the example file, set a few environment variables, and run docker compose up. There are also Kubernetes manifests for larger setups.","category":"Self-Hosting"}

{"question":"What database does Ridgeline use?","answer":"Ridgeline uses PostgreSQL. A single Postgres instance is plenty for personal and small-team use, and migrations run automatically on startup.","category":"Self-Hosting"}
{"question":"Can I import my existing bookmarks?","answer":"Yes. Ridgeline imports browser HTML exports as well as Pocket and Instapaper backups from the import screen.","category":"General"}

{"question":"Does Ridgeline have an API?","answer":"Yes. There's a documented REST API with API keys and rate limiting so you can build your own integrations and automations.","category":"Developers"}

{"question":"How should I back up my data?","answer":"Back up your PostgreSQL database with regular pg_dump snapshots to off-host storage. The docs include scripts and a recommended retention policy, and you should test restores periodically.","category":"Self-Hosting"}
